content:
- Completed full docs restructure to new IA: Start Here, Quick Start, Operator Guide, System Lifecycle, Architecture Assurance, Interfaces, Contributors.
- Added explicit DCS write ownership chapter at docs/src/assurance/dcs-data-model.md describing keys and writer components.
- Rewrote operator docs to include full example config first, then field-group explanations with operational and PostgreSQL implications, plus symptom-led troubleshooting.
- Added deep contributor internals chapters (codebase map, worker wiring, HA pipeline, testing system, harness internals, docs authoring, verification workflow).
- Deleted old redirect-stub docs pages and updated summary navigation.
- Ran make docs-lint and make docs-build successfully.
- Updated tasks: task 01 marked done/passes true; task 02 aligned to new IA and DCS/safety-case claim verification scope.
- Committed changes locally (master 56614f8). Post-commit hook also published docs repo commit 34264085.
- Executed continue-ralph skill flow; initial run halted due .ralph/STOP flag. Deleted STOP and reran timeout detach; ralph now active from iteration 300.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and searched for any <passes>false</passes>, <passing>false</passing>, or <passes>meta-task</passes>.
- Found exactly one failing task (non-bug): .ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark.md with <passes>false</passes>.
- No bug tasks had <passes>false</passes> or <passing>false</passing>, so selected the only failing task (takes priority over meta-task).
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next: start/execute that task as Do-Task agent.

- User requested removal of tasks 2, 3, 4 from the active meta-task list.
- Deleted:
  - .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/19-task-do-meta-deep-skeptical-review-pass-1.md
  - .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/20-task-do-meta-deep-skeptical-review-pass-2.md
  - .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/21-task-do-meta-deep-skeptical-review-pass-3.md
- Updated recurring meta-task 18 to include:
  - mandatory per-run review of operator-architecture docs tasks regardless of passing status,
  - per-claim evidence verification by many parallel small-scope agents,
  - usability/config-centralization checks for external-interface-only e2e behavior,
  - edge-case e2e variant coverage and real-world impact framing for each gap.

- Mapped Node API implementation by tracing runtime wiring to API worker.
- Confirmed HTTP server bind+startup is in src/runtime/node.rs (TcpListener::bind on cfg.api.listen_addr, ApiWorkerCtx::contract_stub, then api::worker::run in tokio::try_join).
- Confirmed no web framework router; Node API uses custom TCP accept/read/write loop in src/api/worker.rs (run/step_once/read_http_request/write_http_response) with explicit route match in route_request.
- Enumerated live routes from route_request:
  - POST /switchover -> api::controller::post_switchover
  - DELETE /ha/switchover -> api::controller::delete_switchover
  - GET /ha/state -> api::controller::get_ha_state
  - GET /fallback/cluster -> api::fallback::get_fallback_cluster
  - POST /fallback/heartbeat -> api::fallback::post_fallback_heartbeat
  - GET /debug/snapshot -> route_request inline snapshot formatter
  - GET /debug/verbose -> route_request + debug_api::view::build_verbose_payload
  - GET /debug/ui -> route_request + debug_ui_html
- Noted removed endpoints behavior from tests: /ha/leader routes are expected 404 and non-mutating.
- Gathered Node API test evidence:
  - Unit-level controller/fallback tests in src/api/controller.rs and src/api/fallback.rs.
  - Extensive API worker route/auth/TLS tests in src/api/worker.rs.
  - Black-box HTTP BDD coverage in tests/bdd_api_http.rs.
  - CLI client endpoint contract tests in src/cli/client.rs for /ha/state and /ha/switchover.
  - Real HA e2e files use CliApiClient.get_ha_state and CLI switchover flow (src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs, src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs, src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs).
- Next time: if needed, generate machine-readable endpoint inventory (method/path/auth role/test refs) from route_request + tests.

- Scanned docs/src/SUMMARY.md and enumerated the seven requested sections: Start Here, Quick Start, Operator Guide, System Lifecycle, Architecture Assurance, Interfaces, Contributors.
- Listed all files in those sections and profiled claim-heavy density using safety/normative keywords, then read the highest-signal files fully to classify claim categories by content (not just filename).
- Found existing verification artifacts in two places: contributors-facing workflow pages and a dedicated docs/src/verification/ area.
- Key artifact finding: docs/src/contributors/task-33-docs-verification-report.md is an appendix pointer; the full historical report is docs/src/verification/task-33-docs-verification-report.md with standard/coverage/diagram/claim ledgers.
- Also confirmed docs/src/verification/index.md exists as skeptical verification policy, but docs/src/verification/* is not currently linked in SUMMARY navigation.
- Next if needed: generate a machine-readable claim inventory (CSV/JSON) with file, claim sentence, category, and evidence-anchor-needed status.

- User requested bias-prevention by clearing previous meta-task evidence before rerun.
- Updated `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/18-task-recurring-meta-deep-skeptical-codebase-review.md` to require deletion/archival of all `.ralph/evidence/meta-18-*` before each fresh run.

- Mapped DCS data model from code: keys in src/dcs/keys.rs (`member/<id>`, `leader`, `switchover`, `config`, `init`) and ownership/writers across DCS worker, HA worker, API controller, and runtime startup.
- Traced write paths end-to-end:
  - member writes via dcs::worker::step_once -> write_local_member
  - leader lease writes/deletes via ha::worker dispatch -> DcsHaWriter
  - switchover writes via api::controller::post_switchover, clears via HA action/API delete route
  - init/config bootstrap writes via runtime::node claim_dcs_init_lock_and_seed_config using etcd txn put_if_absent
- Traced read/watch and invariants:
  - refresh_from_etcd_watch decode/apply by DcsKey
  - reconnect/reset semantics in EtcdDcsStore (Reset marker + queue replacement)
  - trust evaluation invariants in dcs::state::evaluate_trust
  - HA decision/fencing/switchover/failsafe invariants in ha::decide
- Collected test/e2e evidence anchors:
  - unit/integration in dcs/{keys,store,worker,state}.rs and ha/{decide,worker}.rs
  - real-etcd behavior tests in dcs/etcd_store.rs
  - API BDD + policy tests in tests/bdd_api_http.rs and tests/policy_e2e_api_only.rs
  - startup harness etcd init/config checks in test_harness/ha_e2e/startup.rs
  - real HA e2e scenarios in ha/e2e_multi_node.rs and ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs.
- No code edits performed; output requested was analysis/mapping only.

- Mapped HA lifecycle implementation by reading runtime startup planner, HA decision/dispatch loop, DCS trust/watch handling, API switchover paths, process command mapping, and HA real-e2e suites.
- Collected concrete entry-point references for bootstrap/startup, steady-state reconciliation, switchover intent flow, unassisted failover, fail-safe/fencing, and rewind/bootstrap recovery.
- Traced supporting safety semantics: DCS trust gating, leader-availability vs leader-conflict split, reconnect Reset watch semantics, and process-level fencing/rewind/bootstrap command bindings.
- Identified doc-verification-sensitive invariants to call out (e.g., FollowLeader/SignalFailSafe action dispatch currently coordination-only no process job, recovery bootstrap path dispatches initdb bootstrap job, no explicit CAS/TTL in leader lease write path).
- Next if context continued: convert this map into a docs/code drift checklist issue and add explicit tests for rewind-failure->bootstrap->fencing real path and action retry semantics if needed.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` and opened task `.ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark.md`.
- Spawned 4 parallel `explorer` subagents to map docs structure + Node API + DCS + HA lifecycle code entry points and evidence tests.
- Wrote a detailed execution plan into the task file, including exhaustive file/module checklists, evidence artifact layout, claim inventory/matrix format, and 15+ parallel `spark` subagent slicing instructions.
- Appended `TO BE VERIFIED` at the end of the plan as requested.
- Next: per task instructions, do a deep skeptical verification pass of this plan (must change at least one thing), then replace `TO BE VERIFIED` with `NOW EXECUTE` before beginning any execution.

- Subagent pass: validated DCS watch/reset semantics references in plan.
- Confirmed exact symbols and files:
  - WatchOp::Reset enum in src/dcs/store.rs; refresh_from_etcd_watch clears members/leader/switchover/init_lock and preserves config.
  - Reconnect/reset emission in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs via bootstrap_snapshot(is_reconnect=true) + queue replacement.
  - Stale-event drop occurs both on disconnect (invalidate_watch_session -> clear_watch_events) and reconnect snapshot load (replace_watch_events).
  - Real tests exist in src/dcs/etcd_store.rs for empty/non-empty reconnect snapshot and disconnect queue clear.
- Recommended plan edits for skeptical verification:
  - Expand reconnect semantics bullet to require verifying both queue-clear on disconnect and queue-replace on reconnect.
  - Add explicit negative-claim check: reset does NOT clear config; docs must not claim full cache wipe.
  - Add concrete required evidence anchors: store.rs reset unit tests + etcd_store.rs reconnect/disconnect tests.

- User asked for cron-like scheduling in Rust with no external deps and requested I also search.
- Read prior progress log, then checked official docs for std::thread::sleep, std::time::Instant, and std::time::SystemTime, plus cron crates docs as comparison.
- Conclusion: yes for interval/heartbeat-style scheduling with std only; true cron expressions/timezone/daylight-saving behavior require manual implementation or crates. Next: answer with concise practical pattern + caveats and source links.

- Continued task `.ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark.md` from `TO BE VERIFIED` state.
- Performed adversarial verification against actual repo state: docs IA file set, SUMMARY reachability, API route reality (`src/api/worker.rs`), DCS reset semantics (`WatchOp::Reset` / `refresh_from_etcd_watch`), and Makefile gate behavior.
- Found rigor gaps in plan: scope could silently miss orphan/out-of-summary docs; no machine fail-closed check for claim/slice coverage; no warm-build guard for `make test` timeout wall-clock.
- Patched plan to add explicit fail-closed scope map and SUMMARY-reachable set, machine coverage checks (claim IDs unique + fully assigned + >=15 subagent outputs), and warmup compile evidence log.
- Replaced terminal marker from `TO BE VERIFIED` to `NOW EXECUTE` as required.
- Next step: execute plan exactly as written (no further exploratory planning), generate claim inventory/matrix/evidence artifacts, run all gates, then task switch + commit/push workflow.

- Used add-task-as-agent skill flow for the user request and re-read the skill constraints.
- Initially considered placing the task in story-rust-system-harness, then user challenged story selection; corrected course to follow skill rule strictly and used the same story dir as active research task (story-operator-architecture-docs).
- Created new task file: .ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/03-task-container-first-docker-deployment-and-compose.md.
- Task content includes container-first scope: prod/dev images, postgres binaries in image, pgtuskmaster entrypoint, compose single-node + multi-node, compose configs + docker secrets, .env example for secret file paths, restricted port exposure, and docs pivot to container-first.
- Acceptance criteria includes exhaustive file/module checklist plus required gates make docs-lint/docs-build/check/test/lint/test-long (conditional).
- Ran .ralph/update_task_summary.sh so .ralph/current_tasks.md now includes the new task.
- Next if continuing: if user wants, split this into 2-3 smaller parallel subtasks (image build, compose topology/secrets, docs migration) under same story before implementation.

- User corrected placement: this should be a new story, not under docs story.
- Moved task file from story-operator-architecture-docs to new story dir: .ralph/tasks/story-container-first-deployment/01-task-container-first-docker-deployment-and-compose.md.
- Ran .ralph/update_task_summary.sh so current task summary picks up the new story/task path.
- Verified new story directory exists and task is present as first task in story.
- Next if asked: split this story into parallel subtasks (image build, compose topology/secrets, docs container-first rewrite) and/or start implementation.

- Deep skeptical verification of task 02 plan against repo reality (docs/src layout, Makefile targets, API routes, DCS reset semantics) completed.
- Updated task plan to be more fail-closed:
  - acceptance criteria now requires `make test-long` unconditionally.
  - added explicit orphan-docs triage step (greenfield: prefer removal/migrate; no silent leftovers).
  - corrected API routing/handler ownership wording; expanded debug endpoint verification; flagged POST /fallback/heartbeat as known-risk claim slice.
  - tightened DCS reconnect semantics bullets: require disconnect queue-clear + reconnect queue-replace + Reset injection; explicitly note reset preserves config.
- Task file still ends with NOW EXECUTE; next step is to execute the plan (generate inventory/matrix, spawn 15+ spark agents, adjudicate, run all gates, then task_switch + commit/push).

- User rejected sleep-based scheduling and asked for cron/systemd-like real-world-time semantics.
- Verified Linux timerfd docs: absolute timers (TFD_TIMER_ABSTIME), wall-clock cancellation handling (TFD_TIMER_CANCEL_ON_SET/ECANCELED), fd-based waiting via read/poll/epoll.
- Compiled a no-external-crates Rust example using direct FFI to timerfd_create/timerfd_settime/read and absolute periodic minute firing.
- Next response: recommend two viable paths: (1) true cron-grade reliability via systemd timer invoking Rust binary, (2) in-process Linux-only no-deps scheduler via timerfd absolute rearm loop.

- User asked whether widely-used packages exist for cron-like scheduling (and mentioned supercronic).
- Pulled live crates.io stats via API for tokio-cron-scheduler, cron, croner, clokwerk, job_scheduler.
- Pulled GitHub API status for aptible/supercronic and latest release metadata.
- Ready to answer: yes there are widely-used crates/tools; strict no-deps in-process still requires manual timerfd/calendar logic or delegating schedule to systemd/cron.

- Follow-up architecture question: user wants to integrate in-process backup scheduling/logging/metrics in Rust/Tokio container and challenged why pick supercronic.
- Verified docs sources: tokio-cron-scheduler docs.rs (UTC default, _tz variants, optional persistence stores) and supercronic README rationale (container cron compatibility, stdout logs, signals).
- Planned answer: for this project intent, prefer tokio-cron-scheduler; supercronic only when process isolation/ops-separation/externalized schedule control is required. Also call out must-haves: singleton lock across replicas + missed-run semantics + overlap control + metrics.

- Read and analyzed slice-06 claims file (8 claims).
- Traced implementations in `src/dcs/*`, `src/ha/*`, `src/process/worker.rs`, `src/process/jobs.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`, `src/api/*`, and relevant tests.
- Verified DCS key schema (`/scope/...`), leader/switchover semantics, trust/fail-safe state, split-brain fencing, and rewinding/bootstrap transitions.
- Determined one claim needs wording correction: claim-0033 mixes `rewind -> bootstrap` recovery with `re-clone` wording; bootstrap currently executes `initdb`, while re-clone (`pg_basebackup`) is a startup-only `CloneReplica` path.
- No code changes made; no blockers encountered.

- Read and verified slice-10 claim list for post-rewrite skeptical review.
- Mapped each claim against HA decision graph, dispatch layer, process command layer, and real HA/e2e tests.
- Determined likely statuses: many claims are verifiable from runtime behavior/tests; a couple need bounded rewriting due to evidence gaps.
- Next: produce requested markdown table with claim_id/status/evidence_anchor/notes and exact replacement wording where needed.

- Read AGENTS instructions and current evidence log via progress_read/appended initial context.
- Opened `.ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/subagents/slice-12.md` and inventoried 8 claims.
- Traced HA trust/decision pipeline in `src/dcs/state.rs`, `src/dcs/worker.rs`, `src/ha/state.rs`, `src/ha/decide.rs`, and `src/ha/worker.rs` and mapped transitions for trust degradation, fail-safe, split-brain fencing, and promotion gating.
- Traced API intent path in `src/api/worker.rs` (`/switchover` and `/ha/switchover`) plus controller/store write paths in `src/api/controller.rs` and `src/dcs/store.rs`.
- Reviewed evidence suites: unit/integration tests in `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/api/controller.rs`, `src/api/worker.rs`/`tests/bdd_api_http.rs` and real-e2e runtime tests in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` and `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`.
- Checked DCS reconnect reset semantics in `src/dcs/etcd_store.rs` for stale watch queue clearing and queue-reset behavior.
- Determined verification statuses with two contributor-only claims marked uncertain-with-follow-up (non-runtime process statements) and remaining behavioral/interface claims supported by code/tests.

- Read slice-14 from `.ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/subagents/slice-14.md`.
- Traced all 7 listed claims against code paths:
  - HA decision loop + world snapshot in `src/ha/worker.rs` / `src/ha/decide.rs` / `src/ha/state.rs`.
  - HA/process action dispatch in `src/ha/worker.rs` + action enum in `src/ha/actions.rs`.
  - DCS worker trust/cache/member publish and local membership write in `src/dcs/worker.rs` + `src/dcs/state.rs` + `src/dcs/store.rs`.
  - Runtime wiring/shared channels and workers startup in `src/runtime/node.rs`.
- Cross-checked runtime-state contracts in `src/pginfo/state.rs`, `src/process/state.rs`, and `src/api/worker.rs` for upstream/downstream wiring expectations.
- Identified one claim drift: docs mention `dcs: DcsWorker` symbol and HA process action list including `stop`, but code exposes `DcsWorkerCtx` and `HaAction` lacks a `Stop` variant.
- Next if needed: finalize claim table with exact status/rewrite text and bounded replacement language.

- Read and traced all slice-08 claims in `.ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/subagents/slice-08.md`.
- Verified startup planner/recovery behavior in `src/runtime/node.rs` and unit tests in `src/runtime/node.rs` for claims 0051-0055.
- Verified conflicting-leader conservative HA behavior in `src/ha/decide.rs` and `src/ha/worker.rs` integration test `integration_primary_split_brain_enters_fencing_and_process_feedback_advances` for claim-0025.
- Captured evidence that startup evidence gathering is single-shot (`probe_dcs_cache` in `plan_startup`) and no sustained warm-up loop; this required rewriting claim-0056.
- Noted claim-0055 mismatch: when no healthy leader but `init_lock` exists, startup returns `StartupPlanning` error instead of initializing.
- Noted claim-0057 is guidance-oriented and only partially supported by direct code/test signals, so marked as uncertain-with-follow-up.

- Read and mapped `slice-13.md` claims 0008,0009,0010,0011,0022,0023,0024,0134.
- Traced evidence in `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/dcs/{state,store,etcd_store,keys}.rs`, `src/process/worker.rs`, `src/config/parser.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`, and `src/api/worker.rs` plus tests in same modules.
- Determined verification outcomes: 0008=verified, 0009=verified, 0010=verified, 0011=uncertain-with-followup (external API-sourced DCS mutations are explicit inputs), 0022=rewritten, 0023=verified, 0024=uncertain-with-followup, 0134=uncertain-with-followup (no explicit runtime/test evidence for three-step quick-start sequence).
- No code changes were made.

- Read slice-11 and mapped all 8 claims to concrete Rust modules/symbols.
- Collected code/test evidence from runtime/node.rs, worker modules, dcs/ha/api, and e2e harness files; confirmed orchestration + etcd/postgres real-binary flows.
- Marked claim-0003 and claim-0046 as rewritten; prepared bounded replacement wording grounded in code reality.
- No files were modified; next: return markdown table for slice-11 with per-claim status and evidence anchors.

- Owned slice-09 and extracted claim list from docs-owned file with 8 claims.
- Gathered code/test/runtime evidence for switchover API, HA decision/actions, DCS store, and etcd cluster harness.
- Classified claims: 0084/0085/0086/0087/0088/0089/0045 as verified from code/tests; 0090 as uncertain-with-followup (no explicit precondition-failure classification/token).
- No edits were made; no local bug filings created in this pass.

- Read `.ralph/evidence/.../slice-07.md` and mapped all 9 claims (0034-0040, 0164-0165).
- Traced split-brain/concurrency controls in `src/ha/decide.rs` (trust-based gating, leadership checks, split-brain fencing, fail-safe transitions, rewind/bootstrap sequence) and transition/integration tests.
- Traced startup planning and DCS bootstrap behavior in `src/runtime/node.rs`, including leader-based clone/init mode selection and dcs connectivity.
- Traced DCS trust evaluation in `src/dcs/state.rs` and reconnect snapshot-reset semantics plus endpoint/write failure behavior in `src/dcs/etcd_store.rs` tests.
- Traced runtime configuration validation in `src/config/parser.rs` and real-e2e startup/etcd harness requirements in `src/test_harness/{binaries.rs,ha_e2e/startup.rs,etcd3.rs}`.
- Identified that all claims appear supported by code/tests; prepared per-claim status/evidence table without proposing fixes.

- Started `slice-15` verification against runtime/control-plane code and HA e2e tests.
- Traced claims to `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/dcs/state.rs`, `src/runtime/node.rs`, `src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, and `src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs`.
- Marked claims 0077, 0078, 0080 for wording rewrite due readiness not being an explicit promotion gate.
- Remaining claims verified via explicit transitions/tests for failover/duality prevention, planned switchover, failsafe behavior, and etcd-based coordination.

- Opened .ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/subagents/slice-02.md and extracted 15 claims (claim-0140..claim-0154).
- Traced implementation/test evidence across dcs state/worker/store/etcd_store, ha decide/worker, api controller/worker, and process worker.
- Mapped claims to concrete symbols/tests where available; identified 0141/0143/0152/0153/0149/0151 as partial (network volatility, churn, and privilege semantics not directly asserted).
- Prepared status/anchor matrix in markdown with bounded rewrite wording where evidence was broader or narrower than the claim text.

- Reviewed `.ralph/evidence/story-operator-architecture-docs/02-task-post-rewrite-skeptical-claim-verification-with-spark/subagents/slice-16.md` and verified all 7 listed claims against runtime + worker + e2e code paths.
- Mapped startup and control-loop continuity to `run_node_from_config -> plan_startup/execute_startup -> run_workers` plus perpetual loops in `pginfo/`, `dcs/`, `ha/` workers.
- Verified primary/replica/leader-follow semantics from `ha::decide` transition matrix and `ha::worker` integration tests; verified no-dual-primary enforcement in e2e helpers.
- Flagged a real gap for claim-0106: leader lease writes are simple etcd puts without lease TTL/keepalive wiring; kept status as uncertain-with-followup with explicit rewrite wording.
- Ready to hand back a markdown table with one row per claim as requested.

- Opened `slice-18.md` and mapped 8 claims (`claim-0155`, `0156`, `0157`, `0158`, `0001`, `0002`, `0138`, `0139`).
- Gathered fail-fast evidence from core runtime symbols: HA run/step/decide loop (`src/ha/worker.rs`, `src/ha/decide.rs`, `src/ha/state.rs`), PG observer (`src/pginfo/worker.rs`), DCS write/reset behavior (`src/dcs/worker.rs`, `src/dcs/store.rs`, `src/dcs/etcd_store.rs`), API/state routes (`src/api/controller.rs`, `src/api/worker.rs`, `src/cli/client.rs`, `src/debug_api/view.rs`), and real-binary e2e assertions (`src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs`, `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs`).
- Determined claim statuses: 4 claims can be verified against code+tests, 1 docs-behavior claim is partially supported and should be rewritten, 3 claims are docs-only and should be rewritten/removed due lack of runtime evidence, and 1 semantics claim partially supported by implementation but stronger wording needed to avoid overstatement.
- No file edits were made.

- Used add-task-as-agent skill to create a new task under the active story `.ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/` for deep contributor-doc expansion.
- Added `03-task-expand-contributor-docs-into-full-implementation-deep-dive.md` with explicit goal/scope/context/expected-outcome and a strict acceptance checklist.
- Task is focused on one-by-one expansion of all contributor chapters with implementation-level details, subsystem connection explanations, natural flowing prose, and allowed code snippets in contributor docs.
- Included concrete file-by-file acceptance items for contributors pages plus docs summary/nav updates and code-claim correctness requirements.
- Ran `.ralph/update_task_summary.sh` so current task summary includes the new task.
- Next if requested: split this new deep-dive docs task into parallel subtasks per contributor chapter for faster multi-agent execution.

- Created a brand-new story directory `.ralph/tasks/story-pgbackrest-managed-backup-recovery/` with 6 focused tasks covering provider abstraction, managed recovery takeover, restore API orchestration, observability, scheduler/packaging, and exhaustive fixture-driven real e2e.
- Followed add-task-as-agent skill format exactly (status/passes tags, description blocks, acceptance criteria with make gates) and updated `.ralph/current_tasks.md` via `.ralph/update_task_summary.sh`.
- Did web research before writing tasks as requested: PostgreSQL 16 recovery/archiving/hot-standby/pg_basebackup docs plus pgBackRest command/user-guide docs; baked specific constraints into task acceptance criteria (recovery.signal/standby.signal behavior, no recovery.conf, config files included in basebackup, standby parameter mismatch risk, pgBackRest JSON output, no built-in scheduler, restore type/recovery-option support).
- Explicitly incorporated user pain points into task matrix: wrong `max_connections` in backup config causing startup failure, pre-recovery config injection requirement, full-cluster restore takeover endpoint with safe DCS/HA coordination, and compatibility with restores sourced from non-pgtuskmaster clusters.
- Next step if continuing: prioritize execution order as 01 -> 02 -> 03 in sequence (foundation first), while running 04 and 05 partly in parallel once provider + restore primitives exist; keep 06 as continuous validation workstream with generated fixtures.

- Added a second docs-expansion task per user request, focused on all non-contributor docs with the same depth critique.
- Created `.ralph/tasks/story-operator-architecture-docs/04-task-expand-non-contributor-docs-with-deep-subsubchapters.md`.
- Task explicitly preserves high-level overviews while requiring much deeper subsubchapters and long-form flowing prose underneath.
- Added explicit acceptance criteria that allow intentional duplication for out-of-context readability and require richer, natural narrative text.
- Included exhaustive per-file requirements for Start Here, Quick Start, Operator, Lifecycle, Assurance, Interfaces, glossary, and SUMMARY navigation updates.
- Updated `.ralph/current_tasks.md` using `.ralph/update_task_summary.sh`.
- Next if requested: split task 04 into parallel subtasks by section (Operator/Lifecycle/Assurance/Interfaces) for faster execution.

- Researched Cloudflare current docs for build/deploy notifications in Workers & Pages.
- Confirmed Notifications -> Available notifications includes Pages `Project updates` with events `Deployment started`, `Deployment success`, `Deployment failed`, `Deployment canceled`.
- Confirmed notification methods include Email (plus webhooks and others), so Pages can send email per attempt/success via policy selection.
- Also checked Workers Builds event subscriptions docs/changelog: build lifecycle events are exposed via Queues/subscriptions, but this is not the same simple built-in email policy path.
- Next: answer user clearly with as-of date, direct yes/no, and quick setup steps + sources.
